SEARCH
Package server.dao

Class VisitDAO

java.lang.Object
server.dao.VisitDAO
public class VisitDAO extends Object
Data access object for the visit table — one row per party that physically enters a park, by reservation or as a casual walk-in.

Follows the same conventions as ReservationDAO: each method opens a short-lived Connection from DBConnection, runs parameterized statements, and maps rows to VisitDTO. SQL exceptions are logged and surfaced as a null/false/-1 return rather than being propagated, so callers signal failure through the return value.

A visit is open while exited_at IS NULL; closing it stamps the exit time. Live occupancy is therefore the sum of the headcounts of all open visits for a park.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    closeVisit(int visitId)
    Closes an open visit by stamping exited_at = NOW().
    int
    currentOccupancy(int parkId)
    Computes a park's live occupancy: the sum of the headcounts of every open visit (those with exited_at IS NULL).
    common.dto.VisitDTO
    findById(int visitId)
    Looks up a single visit by its primary key — used to echo the row just created back to the gate (the casual path has neither a confirmation code nor, necessarily, a visitor id to look it up by).
    common.dto.VisitDTO
    findOpenByConfirmation(int confirmationCode)
    Finds the open visit (not yet exited) reached by a reservation's confirmation code, joining reservation to resolve the code.
    common.dto.VisitDTO
    findOpenByVisitor(long visitorId)
    Finds the open visit (not yet exited) for a given visitor.

    Records a new (open) visit, letting the database stamp entered_at and leaving exited_at NULL.

  • Constructor Details

    • VisitDAO

      public VisitDAO()

  • Method Details

    • insertVisit

      public int insertVisit(Integer reservationId, int parkId, Long visitorId, int headcount, common.dto.VisitType visitType, Integer priceCents)
      Records a new (open) visit, letting the database stamp entered_at and leaving exited_at NULL.

      The nullable fields are boxed so a Java null becomes a SQL NULL: reservationId is null for a casual walk-in, visitorId for an anonymous party, and visitType/ priceCents are null for a reservation visit (they are persisted only for casual visits, which have no reservation to derive from).

      Parameters:
      reservationId - the backing reservation id, or null for a casual walk-in
      parkId - the park being entered
      visitorId - the visitor's national id, or null if anonymous
      headcount - number of people in the party
      visitType - the casual visit's type, or null for a reservation visit
      priceCents - the casual visit's price in cents, or null for a reservation visit
      Returns:
      the new auto-generated visit id, or -1 if the insert fails

    • findById

      public common.dto.VisitDTO findById(int visitId)
      Looks up a single visit by its primary key — used to echo the row just created back to the gate (the casual path has neither a confirmation code nor, necessarily, a visitor id to look it up by).
      Parameters:
      visitId - the visit identifier to fetch
      Returns:
      the matching VisitDTO, or null if none matches or the query fails

    • findOpenByConfirmation

      public common.dto.VisitDTO findOpenByConfirmation(int confirmationCode)
      Finds the open visit (not yet exited) reached by a reservation's confirmation code, joining reservation to resolve the code. If more than one open visit somehow matches, the most recent wins.
      Parameters:
      confirmationCode - the booking confirmation code presented at the gate
      Returns:
      the open VisitDTO, or null if none is open or the query fails

    • findOpenByVisitor

      public common.dto.VisitDTO findOpenByVisitor(long visitorId)
      Finds the open visit (not yet exited) for a given visitor. If the visitor has more than one open visit, the most recent wins.
      Parameters:
      visitorId - the visitor's national id
      Returns:
      the open VisitDTO, or null if none is open or the query fails

    • closeVisit

      public boolean closeVisit(int visitId)
      Closes an open visit by stamping exited_at = NOW(). The exited_at IS NULL guard makes this idempotent — a visit that is already closed is not re-stamped and the method reports false.
      Parameters:
      visitId - the visit to close
      Returns:
      true if an open visit was closed, false otherwise

    • currentOccupancy

      public int currentOccupancy(int parkId)
      Computes a park's live occupancy: the sum of the headcounts of every open visit (those with exited_at IS NULL). This is the physical head count inside the park right now, not the booking-time availability.
      Parameters:
      parkId - the park to measure
      Returns:
      the number of people currently inside (0 if the park is empty), or -1 if the query fails
